changeSet.author: mherman22
changeSet.comment: Concurrent print requests inserted multiple barcode_label_info rows for the same code (Label.linkBarcodeLabelInfo had no unique constraint and the lookup-then-insert dance is racy). Collapse duplicates into the lowest id, summing num_printed so the print cap stays defensible, before adding the unique constraint.
changeSet.sql:
UPDATE clinlims.barcode_label_info kept SET numprinted = totals.total_printed, lastupdated = NOW() FROM ( SELECT code, SUM(numprinted) AS total_printed, MIN(id) AS keeper_id FROM clinlims.barcode_label_info GROUP BY code HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ) totals WHERE kept.id = totals.keeper_id;
DELETE FROM clinlims.barcode_label_info dup USING ( SELECT code, MIN(id) AS keeper_id FROM clinlims.barcode_label_info GROUP BY code HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ) totals WHERE dup.code = totals.code AND dup.id <> totals.keeper_id;

rollback.comment: Dedup is destructive (collapsed rows can't be split back); rollback is a no-op.

changeSet.author: mherman22
changeSet.comment: The code column is the natural key — one printed barcode value should map 1:1 to a row. Enforcing uniqueness lets concurrent print requests fall through to UPDATE instead of silently spawning duplicate rows that confuse num_printed cap checks.
